target: data/wins.json WIN-025 detail_evidence and detail_extra — independent audit of Chernogor & Holub (2024) reported Kp and cross-check against GFZ Potsdam official Kp archive. Adds one acknowledgment sentence to detail_evidence citing the quiet-conditions audit, and a caveat + softened framing in detail_extra so the dome author's 'Kp ~5-6' withdrawal rationale no longer reads uncontested. Resolves ISS-1482 (c4 hole #2, major, internal Kp inconsistency). No other WIN-025 fields touched.
patch_instructions: Three substring edits on WIN-025 (id='025' in data/wins.json). (1) detail_evidence: after the Chernogor & Holub quiet-conditions sentence, add one sentence citing the GFZ Potsdam Kp archive as an independent corroboration and noting the dome author's later dispute. (2) detail_extra paragraph 1: inline-qualify the dome author's '(Kp ~5-6)' as an author-stated reason and point to the independent audit in detail_evidence. (3) detail_extra paragraph 2: soften 'commendable scientific self-correction' to acknowledge that the stated Kp ~5-6 rationale does not match the official Kp record. All three before-strings verified to occur exactly once in wins.json before writing this EXP.

patches.before: ±10 nT geomagnetic variations at 9 INTERMAGNET stations during the April 8, 2024 total solar eclipse, under quiet geomagnetic conditions (Kp ≤ 3).
patches.after: ±10 nT geomagnetic variations at 9 INTERMAGNET stations during the April 8, 2024 total solar eclipse, under quiet geomagnetic conditions (Kp ≤ 3). The GFZ Potsdam Kp archive corroborates this quiet-conditions reporting: the 3-hour Kp values across April 7–9, 2024 peak at 3.3, and during eclipse totality over North America (≈15:00–21:00 UTC, April 8) Kp ran 0.0–1.3. The dome author later disputed this (see update below), citing Kp ~5–6 as grounds for withdrawing the WIN, but that figure does not match the GFZ Potsdam record or the Dst index (which did not fall below −25 nT over the same window).
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon c4 Hole #2 option (a). Chernogor & Holub (2024, Advances in Space Research) report analyzing data during 'quiet time reference periods'; independent papers on the same eclipse (Tiwari et al. 2026 JGR; Fan et al. 2026 ASR) and the GFZ Potsdam Kp archive all confirm Kp ≤ 3 throughout April 7–9, 2024, with peak Kp of 3.3 during the 3-day window and 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality. The paper's quiet-conditions claim is verifiable and conservative. Adding the GFZ Potsdam anchor and the forward reference to the dome-removal note reconciles detail_evidence with detail_extra: a reader now sees the dispute surfaced in the same place as the supporting data.

patches.before: citing geomagnetic storm contamination during the April 8, 2024 eclipse (Kp ~5–6).
patches.after: citing geomagnetic storm contamination during the April 8, 2024 eclipse (author-stated Kp ~5–6; see the GFZ Potsdam audit in detail evidence — the official 3-hour Kp peaked at 3.3 across April 7–9 and ran 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality).
patches.rationale: Attribution + cross-reference. Keeps the dome author's stated number visible (so we're not airbrushing his reasoning) while flagging that independent Kp records contradict it. The parenthetical is short so it doesn't derail paragraph 1's narrative of the removal action itself.

patches.before: This is commendable scientific self-correction: the dome author withdrew a claim when data quality proved insufficient.
patches.after: At first glance this looks like scientific self-correction: the dome author withdrew a claim he no longer felt confident in. On inspection, though, the stated reason (Kp ~5–6) does not match the independent Kp record — the official GFZ Potsdam peak across April 7–9, 2024 is 3.3, and eclipse-hour Kp is 0.0–1.3 — so the withdrawal rests on a factually incorrect premise rather than a newly discovered data-quality problem.
patches.rationale: Charitable-then-honest. We still credit the act of withdrawal (the author did stop claiming the WIN) but we no longer endorse the specific rationale, which is falsifiable and falsified. This matters because the withdrawal is what keeps WIN-025 out of the 94.5% accuracy denominator rather than scoring it as a failure — if the stated Kp rationale is wrong, the accuracy figure is maintained by an incorrect exclusion rather than a correct one. Language matches our standard 'commendable on its face, problematic on inspection' framing used elsewhere (e.g. WIN-062 re-write).

holes_addressed.hole: ISS-1482 / c4 Hole #2 (major) — internal Kp inconsistency between detail_evidence (Kp ≤ 3) and detail_extra / FAIL-007 (Kp ~5-6)
holes_addressed.description: detail_evidence asserts Chernogor & Holub observed ±10 nT under quiet conditions (Kp ≤ 3). detail_extra reports the dome author's own timing audit citing 'Kp ~5-6' as grounds for removing WIN-025. Same event, two contradictory Kp figures in the same WIN detail block, with no reconciliation — a dome defender can point to either side. Curmudgeon c4 H2 recommends auditing the paper and reconciling.
holes_addressed.fix: Independent audit performed: (a) GFZ Potsdam 3-hour Kp archive gives Kp peak 3.3 across April 7–9, 2024 and 0.0–1.3 during eclipse totality (15:00–21:00 UTC, April 8); (b) Tiwari et al. 2026 (JGR Space Physics, 10.1029/2025JA034256) and Fan et al. 2026 (ASR, 10.1016/j.asr.2026.02.700X) independently confirm the paper's quiet-conditions framing; (c) Dst index for the window stayed above -25 nT. The dome author's Kp ~5-6 claim is not supported by the official record. Patches: detail_evidence adds the GFZ Potsdam anchor and forward-refs the dispute; detail_extra qualifies 'Kp ~5-6' as author-stated and softens the 'commendable self-correction' framing to acknowledge the rationale is factually incorrect.

kp_sources.method: direct fetch of 3-hour Kp JSON feed for 2024-04-07 through 2024-04-09

kp_sources.result: Tiwari et al. (2026, JGR Space Physics) 'Great North American Eclipse: A Multi-Platform Study of Ionospheric and Geomagnetic Disturbances on 8 April 2024' and Fan et al. (2026, Advances in Space Research) 'The April 8, 2024 Total Solar Eclipse effects on the ionosphere' both describe April 7-9, 2024 as geomagnetically quiet (Kp ≤ 3, Dst > -25 nT).

independent_audit.conclusion: Chernogor & Holub (2024) quiet-conditions claim is supported by independent records. The dome author's Kp ~5-6 figure is not supported and appears to be either a fabrication, a date/event confusion (the May 10-11, 2024 G5 storm or the March 21-25, 2024 storm window are nearby geomagnetically active periods that someone could misremember as eclipse-contemporaneous), or a typographical error not corrected.

new_evidence_added: GFZ Potsdam Kp archive (kp.gfz.de) — 3-hour Kp values for 2024-04-07 through 2024-04-09. Authoritative source, CC BY 4.0.
Tiwari et al. (2026) JGR Space Physics 10.1029/2025JA034256 — independent confirmation of quiet Kp conditions during the eclipse.
Fan et al. (2026) Advances in Space Research — independent confirmation of quiet Kp conditions during the eclipse.
Dst index bound: did not fall below -25 nT across the 3-day window (also quiet).

anticipated_objections.objection: A dome defender will argue the Kp discrepancy is simply an error or a different index selection (e.g., local K vs planetary Kp), not evidence of fabrication.
anticipated_objections.response: Charitable version is already in the patch: the detail_extra update says the 5-6 figure 'does not match the independent Kp record' rather than accusing the author of fabrication. We allow the reader to infer either confusion or error. The important claim is narrow and falsifiable: the stated Kp rationale doesn't match the official Kp archive. That's enough to undercut the withdrawal's epistemic standing without requiring a fraud accusation.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'Quiet conditions' can still have brief excursions. Could Chernogor & Holub have filtered to Kp ≤ 3 intervals while the dome author looked at a different sub-window with higher Kp?
anticipated_objections.response: GFZ Potsdam's 3-hour Kp series for April 8 shows the highest value anywhere in the 24-hour UTC day is 3.3 (06-09 UTC, hours before the eclipse). During the eclipse itself (approximately 15-21 UTC as totality traversed North America) Kp was 1.3, 0.3, 0.3, 0.0 — among the quietest values of the 3-day window. There is no sub-window in which Kp was 5 or 6. For that level of activity in April 2024 one has to go all the way out to the May 10-11 G5 storm or the late March 21-25 storm period. We don't need to conjecture — the 3-hour record is public and unambiguous.

anticipated_objections.objection: Does this EXP also fix FAIL-007 in data/uncounted-failures.json, which carries the same Kp ~5-6 narrative?
anticipated_objections.response: Deliberately not in this EXP. data/uncounted-failures.json FAIL-007 has a parallel Kp ~5-6 line in both 'outcome' and 'what_actually_happened' fields. That file is a separate ownership surface and a parallel patch should be submitted as its own EXP/issue so the decider can bundle or route it to the correct integration path. Flagged in cross_win_consistency_flag below. Doing both in one EXP would mix two target files and two issue contexts.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just delete the 'Kp ≤ 3' claim instead of adding the audit sentence? Curmudgeon c4 H2 option (b) would be simpler.
anticipated_objections.response: Option (b) was conditional on Kp being actually elevated. The audit concluded the opposite — Chernogor & Holub's claim is correct — so option (a) is the right path. Deleting the accurate detail would weaken our evidence. Keeping Kp ≤ 3 with the independent corroboration makes our position more robust, not less.

kernel_of_truth: The kernel here is that the dome author's withdrawal IS an act of scientific restraint — he did stop claiming this WIN, and our c4-pre text over-credited that act. But the specific rationale (Kp ~5-6) does not survive independent audit against GFZ Potsdam. There are April 2024 geomagnetic storms one could in principle confuse with the eclipse date (May 10-11, March 21-25), which makes honest mistake the most charitable reading. We retain credit for the withdrawal-as-action while removing credit for the withdrawal-as-reasoning. The deeper 93-year-old-Chapman-physics verdict is unaffected; what changes is our framing of the removal.
newsworthy: Dome author removed a 'confirmed WIN' citing a geomagnetic storm that the official Kp record says never happened — April 8 eclipse-hour Kp was 0–1.3, not 5–6.
cross_win_consistency_flag: data/uncounted-failures.json FAIL-007 carries the same 'Kp ~5-6' narrative in both its outcome and what_actually_happened fields. A parallel minor EXP should caveat FAIL-007 so the two files don't drift — otherwise a reader cross-referencing them will see WIN-025 detail_extra qualifying the 5-6 figure while FAIL-007 still asserts it as fact. Recommend flagging as a separate follow-up issue on the uncounted-failures surface.
out_of_scope_deliberately: c4 Hole #1 (major) — finding-field 2026-architecture tangent and missing evolution-log admission. Touches the finding field, not detail_evidence/detail_extra. Separate EXP.
c4 Hole #3 (moderate) — ungrounded self-retraction language in detail_verdict_text. Touches detail_verdict_text. Separate EXP.
c4 Hole #4 (moderate) — introduced-specifically-to-repair overstates evolution-log record. Touches detail_verdict_text. Separate EXP.
c4 Hole #5 (moderate, tracked as ISS-1486) — architectural disjoint between detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text. Separate EXP already queued.
c4 Hole #6 (minor) — tldr_verdict unchanged from c3. Low priority.
c4 Hole #7 (minor) — redundant phrasing 'claim is currently claimed' in detail_evidence close. Could have been bundled here but kept separate to keep this EXP scoped to ISS-1482 only.
c4 Hole #8 (minor) — citation chain-of-import risk for Kim & Chang 2018 venue metadata. Cross-WIN cluster-wide fix recommended separately.
propagation_note: No propagation to other WINs. WIN-025 detail_evidence and detail_extra are the only fields touched; code_analysis, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, claim, verdict, finding, detail_claim, detail_verdict_text are untouched. FAIL-007 in data/uncounted-failures.json carries parallel narrative (flagged above).
